query: What is multiple evanescent white dot syndrome?
ground_truth: Multiple evanescent white dot syndrome (MEWDS) is a rare chorioretinopathy causing unilateral visual impairments and characterized by multifocal yellow-white spots in the retina.

candidates.contents: Passage: Text: Multiple evanescent white dot syndrome was first described in 1984 as a rare, sudden onset of unilateral chorioretinopathy, with the predominant sign being multifocal yellow-white spots throughout the retina .
Passage: Abstract: OBJECTIVE: To describe an unusual cluster of multiple evanescent white dot syndrome encountered within a 3-month period.
Title: Multimodal Imaging in an Unusual Cluster of Multiple Evanescent White Dot Syndrome

The clinical spectrum of MEWDS has expanded over the years to include bilaterality and recurrences or an atypical presentation involving the fovea without the white spots .
Symptoms include acute onset of decreased visual acuity unilaterally accompanied in most cases by photopsia and scotomata.

query: How are the influenza tests carried out?
ground_truth: Influenza tests use respiratory samples like swabs and washes, collected within 4 days of illness, and may include lower respiratory secretions or RT-PCR for detection.

candidates.contents: Molecular testing, including RT-PCR for influenza viruses can also be performed on bronchoalveolar lavage fluid if collected for the testing of other pathogens.

Passage: In hospitalized patients receiving invasive mechanical ventilation in whom influenza is suspected, but not yet diagnosed, influenza testing should be performed on endotracheal aspirate specimens instead of those collected from the upper respiratory tract .
